title: LINN COUNTY CRISIS LINE - 211info
text: LINN COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH ALBANY LINN COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Offers initial crisis screening assessment, telephone triage, crisis intervention, pre-commitment investigation, and hospital liaison services. Also offers in-person crisis intervention services during the day. 445 3rd Avenue SW Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week In-person crisis services: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 967-3866 ( 1) Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Crisis Line (541) 967-3866 (press 1) Toll-free (800) 304-7468 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or walk in for crisis services FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Linn County Services: General Crisis Intervention Hotlines In Person Crisis Intervention Mental Health Crisis Lines Suicide Prevention Hotlines Other Services Offered At This Location: MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - ADULT OUTPATIENT SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - CHILD OUTPATIENT SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH SUPPORT SERVICES - EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. LINN COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH ALBANY
